title: ranunculus cornutus var. leiocarpus
intro: Ranunculus cornutus var. leiocarpus, also known as the bristly buttercup, is a lesser-known variety of the buttercup plant, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Ranunculus cornutus var. leiocarpus include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, making it a valuable remedy in traditional folk medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into infusions, tinctures, or ointments, which can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed in moderation to reap its medicinal benefits.
